title: Personnel Screening — Contractor Badge Activations Before Background Check Complete

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.6.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.6.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.17: Personnel Screening — Contractor Badge Activations Before Background Check Complete.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.17' running on pacs:access; hr_screening.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access action=grant earliest=-90d
| lookup hr_screening.csv badge_id OUTPUT screening_complete_date
| eval screen_epoch=strptime(screening_complete_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(screen_epoch) OR _time < screen_epoch
description: Joins physical access grant events to HR screening completion dates to reduce insider risk from premature access.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes pacs:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align badge_id keys; (2) emergency access workflow exception lookup; (3) annual ISO internal audit evidence. Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pacs:access`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Lenel/CCure HEC, HR CSV.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pacs:access`; `hr_screening.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align badge_id keys; (2) emergency access workflow exception lookup; (3) annual ISO internal audit evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access action=grant earliest=-90d
| lookup hr_screening.csv badge_id OUTPUT screening_complete_date
| eval screen_epoch=strptime(screening_complete_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(screen_epoch) OR _time < screen_epoch
```

Understanding this SPL

**Personnel Screening — Contractor Badge Activations Before Background Check Complete (A.6.1)** — Joins physical access grant events to HR screening completion dates to reduce insider risk from premature access.

Documented **Data sources**: `pacs:access`; `hr_screening.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Lenel/CCure HEC, HR CSV. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype=pacs:access, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **screen_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(screen_epoch) OR _time < screen_epoch` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Time chart
grandmaExplanation: We watch for joins physical access grant events to hr screening completion dates to reduce insider risk from premature access. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
